subcategory: Annual revenues from consortium's sale of Iranian oil abroad
notes: Cited by the author to Mark Gasiorowski, U.S. Foreign Policy and the Shah (1991), 102-103, and Mary-Ann Heiss (1994) -- this RAC report is a secondary synthesis citing those primary/scholarly sources, not itself a primary document.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.1

subcategory: Second Seven Year Plan original budget
notes: Development plan implemented by the Plan Organization (Sazman-e Barnama) under Abdolhossein Ebtehaj.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.2

subcategory: Second Seven Year Plan revised (raised) budget
notes: Budget was later raised from the original $930 million/70 billion rials figure.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.2

subcategory: Ford Foundation grant to support the Harvard Advisory Group (HAG)
notes: Grant for a three-year period; also meant to supplement salaries of US-trained Iranians returning to work at the Plan Organization's Economic Bureau.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.6

subcategory: Estimated excess of Iran's state spending over revenues by September 1962 (Hansen/Farmanfarmaian projection)
notes: US Embassy considered the estimate optimistic; indicated a looming financial crisis once foreign-exchange reserves became depleted.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.6

subcategory: Cumulative Plan Organization spending, with difficult-to-measure achievement
notes: "After several years and $505 million, it was difficult to measure what the Plan Organization had achieved."
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.7

subcategory: Third Development Plan targeted average annual GNP growth rate
notes: Goal articulated by Hansen and Farmanfarmaian for the Third Plan, contrasted with the more ad-hoc "shopping list" approach of the Second Plan.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.9

subcategory: Balance of payments crisis following a bad harvest, requiring emergency IMF financing
notes: Austerity measures followed: state salaries frozen, additional development projects scrapped. No specific dollar amount given in the source text for the IMF financing itself.
citation: Gregory Brew, "Economic Expertise and Rural Improvement in Iran, 1948-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2017, p.9

subcategory: Iranian population share living and working as tenant farmers under the land-tenure system
notes: Cited by the author to Daniel Craig, "The Impact of Land Reform on an Iranian Village," Middle East Journal 32, no. 2 (1978), p. 144.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.2

subcategory: Villages within the Shah's own landholdings ordered divided among the local population
notes: Official edict (firman) issued by the Shah, distributed via the Amlak agency without cost to recipients.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.3

subcategory: Villages targeted for land redistribution under the 1952 Point Four-incorporated program
notes: A pilot-project approach; program was scrapped by Prime Minister Mossadegh's rival legislation by early 1953.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.3

subcategory: Villages targeted for land redistribution under Motheral's proposed five-year program
notes: Proposed timeline from the 1957 Motheral Report; teams of US and Iranian technicians, transitioning to fully Iranian-staffed and US-advisory by end of period.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.7

subcategory: Industrial Development Bank of Iran established (later Industrial and Mining Development Bank)
notes: Established as an offshoot of Iran's Bank Melli with private capital from both Iran and the US, urged by both governments as an expansion of development-banking activity called for in the Motheral Report.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.6

subcategory: Central Bank of Iran established with consultation of US-educated economists
notes: Took an active role in financing development projects including land reform moving into the 1960s.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.6

subcategory: Iranians the Shah claimed the White Revolution would "emancipate"
notes: The Shah's own framing/claim for the land-reform-centered White Revolution program, not an independently verified figure.
citation: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.9
